channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's re-appointed prime minister Lecornu calls for calm amid political chaos
item.description: L'HAY-LES-ROSES, France (AP) — France’s newly re-appointed prime minister acknowledged Saturday that there weren’t ″a lot of candidates″ for his job — and that he might not last long in the post given the country’s deep political divides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump envoy Witkoff visits IDF base in northern Gaza as peace deal advances, thousands return home
item.description: White House Special Envoy Steve Witkoff visited an IDF base in northern Gaza on Saturday as thousands of Palestinians were seen returning to their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins and US troops land in Israel as ceasefire holds
item.description: Tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians are returning to their neighborhoods in Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is now in its second day.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Kareem Hana, Sam Metz, Sarah El Deeb And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza were starting to walk north to return to their homes Friday, after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that its soldiers were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday for her struggle to achieve a democratic transition in the South American nation, winning recognition as a woman “who keeps the flame of democracy burning amid a growing darkness.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV, REGINA GARCIA CANO and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize bets on Polymarket raise leak concerns, prompts probe
item.description: The last-minute bets, first reported by local outlets, are a cause for concern for the Norwegian officials in charge of awarding the honor
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Dutch far-right politician Wilders fears he may have been a target of Belgian attack plotters
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Dutch far-right politician Geert Wilders said Friday that he’s suspending campaigning for national elections later this month following reports that he was a possible target of a suspected plot in Belgium to kill politicians using a drone carrying explosives.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump in talks with Putin on reuniting Ukrainian children with families
item.description: First lady Melania Trump said Friday she has been in communication with Russian President Vladimir Putin about reuniting Ukrainian children displaced by war with their families.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize chair responds to Trump's pressure campaign
item.description: The chair of the Nobel Peace Prize committee responded Friday to President Trump's public pressure campaign to win the award, telling reporters that the deliberation process for choosing the awardee prioritizes courage and integrity.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israel's military says Gaza ceasefire now in effect
item.description: Israel's military announced early Friday that the ceasefire in Gaza is now in effect, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's Cabinet approved the first phase of President Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Burkina Faso rejects proposal to accept deportees from the US
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Burkina Faso says it has refused a proposal from the Trump administration to accept deportees from the United States. The West African country was asked whether it would accept non-citizens expelled by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Citing unease over graft, Japan's Komeito leaves the longstanding ruling coalition headed by the LDP
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The head of Japan's Komeito says it is leaving the ruling coalition headed by the Liberal Democratic Party due to concerns over corruption, in a major setback for the woman who hopes to become the country's next prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan's president pledges to build air defense system in face of China threat
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan will accelerate the building of a “Taiwan Shield” or “T-Dome” air defense system in the face of the military threat from China, its leader said Friday. President Lai Ching-te also pledged to raise defense spending to more than 3% of GDP and to reach 5% by 2030.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean investigators indict Unification Church leader over alleged ties to ex-first lady
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators on Friday indicted the 82-year-old leader of the Unification Church, Hak Ja Han, over allegations that she instructed church officials to bribe the wife of jailed former President Yoon Suk Yeol and a conservative lawmaker close to him.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: Philippine seismologists say that a new offshore earthquake with a preliminary 6.8 magnitude hit the same southern region struck earlier by an earthquake with a magnitude of 7.4.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Manchester synagogue attacker 'pledged allegiance to Islamic State,' police say
item.description: The suspect in the deadly attack on a synagogue in Manchester allegedly pledged allegiance to the Islamic State in a call to emergency dispatchers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Hamas Gaza militias reject terror group, declare support for Trump’s peace plan
item.description: Support for a peace plan grows as negotiators work in Cairo to seal the deal, with Gazan militias backing Trump’s proposal amid fighting and calls for new local governance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Priscilla nears major hurricane status in Pacific as tropical storm swirls in Atlantic
item.description: Just off the west coast of Mexico, Priscilla was spinning with maximum sustained winds around 105 mph and moving northwest at 9 mph.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu, on 2-year mark of Oct. 7 Hamas terror attack, says Israel 'not broken,' vows to bring hostages home
item.description: Prime Minister Netanyahu marked two years since Hamas’ Oct. 7 assault in a statement issued on X, honoring the victims and vowing Israel’s war for survival continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cuban-led caravan aims for Mexico City as Trump policies deter migrants from US
item.description: Cuban migrants in Mexico choose Mexico City over U.S. destinations as President Donald Trump's strict immigration policies influence regional migration trends.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rare ancient artwork vanishes from tomb in Egypt: Report
item.description: The artwork, a 4,000-year-old limestone relief, was reported to have disappeared over the weekend from a Fifth Dynasty tomb.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Snowstorm traps hundreds of hikers on Mount Everest during China's national holiday
item.description: About 350 hikers had reached a meeting point in Tingri country and rescuers were in contact with another 200, state broadcaster CCTV said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel and Hamas prepare for negotiations in Egypt ahead of possible Gaza ceasefire
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel and Hamas prepared for indirect negotiations in Egypt on Monday, as hopes for a possible ceasefire in Gaza grew after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said a hostage release could be announced this week. Tuesday marks two years since the Hamas attack that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, WGN 9
